court_or_jurisdiction.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF KARNATAKA AT BENGALURU

facts_background.text: The appellant had taken a personal loan from the respondent company. Due to financial hardship, the appellant defaulted on EMIs for three months. The respondent initiated recovery proceedings and imposed a penalty charge of INR 75,000.

issues.text: Whether the penalty imposed by the respondent was excessive and unfair. Whether the recovery proceedings violated principles of natural justice.

arguments.text: The appellant argued that the penalty was arbitrary and disproportionate. The respondent contended that the penalty was as per the loan agreement terms.

decision_outcome.text: The appeal is partly allowed. The penalty of INR 75,000 is reduced to INR 20,000. The recovery proceedings shall continue subject to this modification.

key_reasons.text: The imposed penalty appears disproportionate to the actual loss suffered.

clauses_obligations.text: The penalty clause allowed for reasonable compensation but not excessive charges.

confirm_or_ask.item: The respondent shall refund the excess penalty amount within 30 days.

what_to_negotiate.item: The loan agreement terms, the penalty clause and the actual loss suffered.

evidence_to_keep.item: The loan agreement and the recovery proceedings.

consult_lawyer_triggers.item: Whether the penalty imposed was excessive and unfair, whether the recovery proceedings violated principles of natural justice.

disclaimer: This report is for informational purposes only and is not legal advice. Legal outcomes depend on full facts, evidence, and jurisdiction. Consider consulting a qualified lawyer.
